title: 2D inverse heat conduction: recover conductivity k(x,y) from sparse sensors
governing_equations: \nabla\!\cdot(k(x,y)\nabla T)=q\ \text{on}\ (0,1)^2,\ T|_{\partial\Omega}=0;\ \text{recover}\ k\ \text{from sparse }T\text{ obs};\ k^*=1+\tfrac12\sin\pi x\sin\pi y
expected_band: recovered conductivity field k(x,y); relative-L2 vs k* ~1e-2-5e-2 (loosest where |grad T| is small, near the boundary)

variants.note_en: Conductivity field recovered from ~100 sparse noisy T sensors; relative-L2 vs the exact k*.
variants.note_es: Campo de conductividad recuperado desde ~100 sensores T dispersos y ruidosos; L2-relativo vs el k* exacto.
